articles.author: Explore Omaha
articles.title: The Skill That Requires No Degree and No Permission
articles.body:
She had the corporate job. She had the title. She had the schedule, the commute, and the benefits package.

And she was completely unfulfilled.

Alexis made a decision that a lot of people talk about but very few actually follow through on. She walked away from the traditional 9-to-5 and stepped into the world of sales — not because she had a plan, not because she had training, but because she had a gift she had not yet recognized: the ability to connect with people and communicate her value clearly.

Her first deal closed for $5,000. No script. No formal coaching. No prior experience.

That moment produced a sentence that every parent, every entrepreneur, and every person who has ever felt underpaid for their talent needs to hear:

*"My money piece is my mouthpiece."*

---

## The Skill That Requires No Degree and No Permission

One of the most powerful things about sales as a career path — and as a life skill — is that it operates on a completely different set of rules than the traditional economy.

No credit score required. No degree required. No manager has to approve your raise. You are not trading hours for a salary someone else decided you deserve.

You are paid for what you produce.

In a conversation on the Circle of Greatness podcast with host Nehemiah Davis, Alexis laid this out plainly: if you learn to sell, you will never go broke. That is not hype. That is economics. Every business on earth needs someone who can communicate value and close a transaction. The person who can do that will always have options.

For parents who are juggling household responsibilities while trying to build something on the side, this matters enormously. You do not have four years to go back to school. You do not have a spare $50,000 lying around for a business investment. But if you can learn to have a confident conversation — to clearly explain why your product or service makes someone's life better — you have a foundation that no one can take from you.

---

## Four Weeks to Your First Client

Alexis has a system. And it is not complicated.

Her students typically go from zero to closing their first client in about four weeks. The reason that timeline works is not because the material is easy — it is because she forces action at every stage.

Consuming information feels productive. Watching another training, reading another book, saving another post — these activities feel like progress. But the breakthrough never comes from consuming. It comes from doing.

She builds action-oriented tasks into her curriculum from day one. Her students are in the field applying what they have learned before they feel ready. That discomfort is where the skill actually forms.

This is a principle that transfers well beyond sales. Whatever you are building — a business, a brand, a side income — the gap between learning and earning closes only when you start doing the thing before you feel fully prepared.

---

## Why Video Beats Every Other Cold Outreach Method

Here is a practical tip that Alexis shares with her students that separates the people who get responses from the ones who get ignored.

Stop sending cold text messages. Send video instead.

A short video — even thirty to sixty seconds recorded on your phone — allows a potential client to see your face, hear your voice, and sense your personality before they ever agree to a call. It breaks through the noise in a way that a text message simply cannot.

Think about your own inbox. How many cold texts or emails do you delete without reading? Now think about the last time someone sent you a personal video. Did you watch it? Almost certainly yes.

The goal is not to be flashy. The goal is to be human. When a prospect can see that you are a real person who genuinely believes in what you offer, the barrier to a conversation drops significantly.

For parent entrepreneurs who may feel like they do not have a "professional" setup — you do not need a studio. You need a phone, decent lighting, and something real to say.

---

## How to Handle "I Need to Think About It"

Every person in business will eventually hear some version of "I need to think about it." Most people handle this moment wrong — they either push too hard and lose the relationship, or they back off completely and lose the sale.

Alexis uses a three-step framework she calls Validate, Elevate, and Navigate.

*Validate* — Acknowledge what the prospect is feeling. "That makes complete sense. This is an important decision and I respect that you are taking it seriously."

*Elevate* — Honor their thoughtfulness. "The fact that you are not just jumping into things tells me you are intentional about how you invest. That is actually what I look for in a client."

*Navigate* — Gently move the conversation toward a solution. "Can I ask what would need to be true for this to feel like the right move for you right now?"

Notice what is not in this framework: pressure. There is no urgency tactic, no artificial deadline, no manipulation. This works because people do not resist being understood — they only resist being sold to. When a prospect feels genuinely heard, the conversation opens back up naturally.

---

## The Valley of Despair Is Not the End

Perhaps the most important thing Alexis shared in the entire conversation is something most people never warn you about before you start something new.

She calls it the valley of despair.

It works like this. You start a new business model or pursue a new income stream. Things feel exciting and full of possibility. Then, inevitably, it gets hard. Results slow down. Doubt creeps in. The excitement fades. And at that exact moment — the moment you are about to break through — most people quit.

They go find the next shiny opportunity and start over. Then they hit the same valley again. Then they quit again. And the cycle repeats until they conclude that nothing works, that the opportunity was a scam, that success is for other people.

Alexis's message is simple: the valley is not a sign that you chose wrong. It is a sign that you are exactly where growth happens. The people who come out on the other side of that valley are the ones who figured out what was not working, made adjustments, and stayed in the game.

Her advice for beginners comes down to three commitments:

1. Invest in your own skill set before you worry about the market. Stocks, crypto, and real estate all require capital. Your mouthpiece requires practice.

2. Find your community. You cannot reach your potential in isolation. Being around people who are winning changes what you believe is possible for yourself.

3. Remember that winning is the cure for burnout. As Alexis put it: "You are never tired when you are winning." The exhaustion most people feel is not from working too hard — it is from working hard without results. The answer is not to rest more. It is to get better until the wins start coming.

---

## The Bottom Line

If you are a parent who is also trying to build something — whether that is a sales career, a small business, a service, or a brand — the foundation Alexis describes is available to you right now. No degree. No credit score. No permission from anyone.

Your ability to communicate, connect, and clearly express the value of what you offer is worth more than most people ever realize. The question is whether you are willing to develop it.

For Alexis, investing in that skill changed everything. It can do the same for you.

---

*You might also like: "What a Demand Letter Can Do for Your Business — And Why You Should Have One Ready" from our Know Your Rights series.*

---

*Conversations like this one are happening regularly on Nobility TV Network — a streaming platform built around content that actually moves the needle for families and entrepreneurs. Explore the network: https://nobilitytvnetwork.com*

articles.author: Explore Omaha
articles.title: Why You Feel Behind (And Why That Feeling Is Lying to You)
articles.body:
It is June. And if you made a goal in January that has quietly lost momentum, you are not alone — and more importantly, you are not behind.

What you actually need is not a new goal. You need a new target for the same goal you already set.

That distinction matters more than it sounds. Michele O'Neil, a digital business coach who built a multi-million dollar online business without ever sending a cold DM, calls this the realignment. And she makes the case that the month of June is the single best time to do it.

Here is why the timing works: if you realign right now, you still have all of July, August, September, October, November, and December — six full months — to finish what you started. The goal does not die in June. It gets relaunched.

---

## Why You Feel Behind (And Why That Feeling Is Lying to You)

The habit most entrepreneurs are stuck in is not failing — it is goal-switching.

Something gets hard. Progress slows. The excitement of January fades. So the response is to set a new goal, start fresh, find a new system, try a new approach. It feels productive. It is actually the reason nothing ever gets finished.

Michele is direct about this: never change the goal until you achieve the goal. What you change are the targets — the specific milestones and approaches you use to reach it. The goal itself stays fixed.

When you finally achieve that one goal you refused to quit on, something shifts permanently. You have proven to yourself that you are capable of finishing. That proof compounds. The next goal becomes easier not because the goal is smaller but because you are different — you are now someone who finishes.

That is the version of yourself that becomes unstoppable.

---

## Clearing the Limiting Beliefs First

Before the three questions, Michele addresses the three most common reasons people talk themselves out of building an online business. All three are false.

The first is tech. Michele makes six figures a month and does not like tech, does not want to learn tech, and has built her systems around avoiding it. If that is the wall stopping you, it is not a real wall.

The second is personality. Michele is an extreme introvert. She built her business without cold outreach, without DMs, without working a room. The idea that you have to be loud, charismatic, or socially tireless to build an online income is simply not accurate.

The third is audience size. She started from zero on TikTok in September 2022 — complete beginner, no following, no idea what she was doing. The follower count did not build her business. The system did.

Remove those three objections from your thinking and a very different set of possibilities opens up.

---

## The Framework: Three Questions That Actually Move the Needle

Michele's realignment process comes down to three questions. They take less than an hour to work through and they will show you exactly where your next six months need to go.

*Question One: What worked?*

Most people skip this entirely because they are so focused on what is not working that they cannot see what already is. Michele pushes back hard on this tendency.

If you posted content and got any views — five, fifty, five hundred — your content is working. It just needs to work better. That is a completely different problem than content that is not working at all, and it has a completely different solution.

Stack your wins. Write them down. What you focus on, you get more of. When you identify what has already been working since January, you now have something to do more of — and more of what already works is exactly how momentum builds.

A 7-second video Michele filmed in Costa Rica generated $8,000 in sales. She knows that because she tracked it. She knew it worked, so she did more of it. That is the entire model.

*Question Two: What did not work?*

Here is where honesty matters. But Michele draws a careful line between what genuinely failed and what simply needed more consistency, more intention, or more repetition.

What did not work was probably not your business model. What did not work was likely the inconsistency. The stinking thinking. The days you did not look at your goal. The weeks you spent consuming instead of executing.

If you got one lead since January, what you are doing is working. Figure out what you said or did to get that one lead. Say it — or do it — a hundred thousand more times.

She is not being glib about that. She genuinely believes, based on her own data and the results of her students, that people dramatically over-complicate what it takes to build an online income. The system is simple. The hard part is staying in it long enough to let it work.

*Question Three: What did you never start?*

This is the most important question and the most uncomfortable one.

How many goals did you set in January that you never actually began? Not goals you tried and struggled with — goals you set and then simply never took the first step toward.

This is where the realignment lives. Not in optimizing what you are already doing. In finally starting the thing you put off.

---

## Build Your Business Off Data, Not Emotion

The reason Michele can make decisions quickly and confidently — the reason she is not paralyzed by algorithm changes or slow weeks — is because she tracks everything.

She spends 10 minutes per week writing down her data: how many leads, how many sales, what she is going to do differently next week. That is it. Ten minutes. From that practice, she is never operating off of hope or frustration. She is operating off of information.

She describes the alternative — posting and praying, hoping the algorithm favors you, making decisions based on how you feel about your content — as the source of most of the pressure and anxiety entrepreneurs carry. There is no peace in that approach. There is no strategy in it either.

Data removes the emotion from the equation. When a video underperforms, it is a data point, not a verdict on your worth. When something works, you know exactly why, and you do more of it.

This is how she built a multi-million dollar business without sending a single DM. Not hustle. Not volume. Not grinding through cold outreach. A system, tracked consistently, adjusted based on what the numbers actually said.

---

## The Leadership Lid

One more concept Michele shares that is worth sitting with.

There is a John C. Maxwell book called The Law of the Lid. The premise is that your results will never exceed your leadership level. It is not about strategy, not about the platform, not about your niche. It is about whether you have done the internal work to lead yourself well.

If the idea of being fully responsible for your income feels like pressure rather than peace, Michele says that is a signal — not to quit, but to invest in your leadership development. The strategies are learnable. The mindset that sustains them through the hard months is the real work.

Winners never quit on the goal. Quitters never win.

The only reason you will not reach your goals in 2026 is because you stop before you get there.

---

## Take the Next Step

Michele O'Neil's Community Builders program teaches the system behind everything covered here — the data tracking, the content strategy, the realignment framework — in a step-by-step format designed for real people with real schedules.

If you are ready to stop setting new goals and start finishing the one that matters, this is where to go:

[Click here to learn more →](https://www.skool.com/business-and-grant-mentorship-9581/about?ref=b26a59d7ef0b4eca9f8265176094fab3)

---

*You might also like: "You Are Not Behind — 12 Quick Money Wins for Parents Who Are Worried Right Now" from our Family Finance series.*

---

*Michele coaches inside Nobility TV Network's growing community of family-focused entrepreneurs. Explore the platform: https://nobilitytvnetwork.com*

articles.author: Explore Omaha
articles.title: What a Value Ladder Actually Is
articles.body:
Here is a question most online entrepreneurs never ask themselves:

What happens after the sale?

Most business models are built around getting the customer. The offer, the funnel, the content strategy, the outreach — everything points toward one moment: the transaction. And once that transaction happens, the model essentially resets. Find the next customer. Make the next sale.

That is an exhausting way to build a business. And it leaves an enormous amount of value — and income — on the table.

The entrepreneurs who build lasting, scalable online income have figured out something different. They are not just making sales. They are building relationships inside environments specifically designed to deepen over time. And one of the most powerful platforms for doing exactly that is Skool.

---

## What a Value Ladder Actually Is

The concept of a value ladder is straightforward. Instead of offering one product at one price point, you create a progression — a series of offers that rise in value, depth, and price as a customer's trust in you deepens and their results grow.

At the base of the ladder, the entry point is low or even free. The goal is not to maximize revenue from that first interaction. The goal is to deliver enough value that the customer naturally wants to go deeper. As they move up the ladder, each step represents a higher level of access, personalization, or transformation — and a correspondingly higher price point.

The genius of this model is that your best customers are not strangers. They are people who already bought from you, already got results, and are actively looking for the next level of support you can offer.

A Skool community is uniquely built for this.

---

## Why Skool Works Where Other Platforms Fall Short

There are dozens of platforms that allow you to sell courses, host memberships, or build email lists. Most of them do one thing reasonably well. Skool does something different — it combines community, courses, and engagement in a single environment where all three reinforce each other.

Here is why that matters for a value ladder specifically.

On most platforms, a customer buys a course and disappears into a private dashboard to watch videos alone. There is no ongoing relationship. There is no reason to return after the content is consumed. The transaction ends the relationship rather than deepening it.

On Skool, the community is the product. When someone joins — whether through a free community or a paid membership — they enter an environment where conversation is happening, questions are being answered, wins are being shared, and connections are forming. That ongoing engagement is what keeps people in the ecosystem long enough to move up the value ladder naturally.

The platform also has a built-in discovery feature. Free communities appear in Skool's search results, meaning you can attract new potential customers without paid advertising, without constantly producing content for an algorithm, and without cold outreach. The community markets itself as it grows.

---

## The Layers of Revenue a Skool Value Ladder Creates

The ascending value ladder inside a Skool community typically looks something like this:

*Layer One — Free Community* The entry point. No cost to join. This is where you demonstrate your expertise, build trust, and give potential customers a genuine taste of the transformation you offer. A well-run free community attracts new members daily through organic discovery on the platform. Every person who joins is a potential customer at a future layer.

*Layer Two — Paid Membership* The first revenue layer. Members pay a recurring monthly or annual fee for access to deeper content, more direct interaction with you, live sessions, accountability, or a more intimate group environment. Because the relationship was built at the free level first, the conversion feels natural rather than forced. These members already know you deliver value.

*Layer Three — Course or Curriculum* A structured, self-paced learning experience layered on top of the community. Members who want to go through a proven system — not just consume live content — purchase access to a course housed directly inside Skool. No separate platform. No broken links. Everything lives in one place.

*Layer Four — High-Ticket Coaching or Mastermind* The top of the ladder. A small, selective group of your most committed customers who want direct access, personalized feedback, and the highest level of accountability. This layer carries the highest price point and requires the deepest relationship — which, by this point, has been built across multiple interactions inside the community over weeks or months.

Each layer serves the customer at a different level of readiness and investment. And because they all exist inside a single Skool environment, moving from one layer to the next is seamless. There is no new platform to sign up for, no different login, no disruption to the relationship.

---

## The Recurring Revenue Advantage

The most powerful financial aspect of this model is not any single layer. It is the recurring nature of the middle layers.

When someone pays a monthly membership fee to be part of your community, that revenue does not reset to zero at the end of the month. It renews. It compounds. As more members join and existing members stay, the monthly baseline income grows without requiring a proportional increase in your effort or output.

This is the difference between a business that earns based on how hard you work any given month and a business that earns based on the asset you have built over time.

A community is a business asset. It appreciates. It generates income while you sleep, while you are with your family, and while you are doing the work that only you can do at the higher levels of your ladder.

---

## Engagement Is the Metric That Drives Everything

One insight that separates the Skool communities generating serious recurring income from the ones that stall out is this: the size of your community is far less important than its engagement level.

An engaged community of five hundred members who show up consistently, post regularly, and trust the environment will generate more revenue, more referrals, and more upward movement on the value ladder than a dormant community of ten thousand passive members who joined once and never returned.

Engagement is built through consistency, genuine responsiveness, and a culture where members feel seen. When people feel that their questions matter, that their progress is noticed, and that the space is genuinely invested in their success, they stay. And the longer they stay, the more they invest — in time, in trust, and in the higher tiers of your ladder.

This is not a content formula. It is a leadership practice.

---

## The False Belief That Keeps Most People on the Sideline

The most common reason people delay building a community is the belief that they need to be further along first.

More followers. More credibility. More content. More proof.

This belief inverts the actual sequence. Community is not the result of having built an audience. Community is how you build one. The people who started a year ago with nothing and now run thriving Skool communities did not wait until they felt ready. They started, served consistently, and let the compounding do its work.

Hesitation halts your harvest. The best time to plant was a year ago. The second best time is now.

---

## A Platform Built for the Long Game

What makes Skool particularly well-suited for a value ladder is that it rewards longevity. The longer your community runs, the more content it accumulates, the deeper the relationships grow, the higher you appear in discovery, and the more natural referrals you receive from satisfied members.

This is not a platform designed for viral moments or overnight results. It is designed for entrepreneurs who are building something that lasts — a recurring income model tied to genuine relationships and real transformation, not content that disappears in 24 hours.

For a parent entrepreneur who needs their business to work around their life rather than consume it, that distinction matters enormously.

---

## Learn From Someone Who Has Already Built It

The concepts in this post are publicly available. The frameworks exist. The platform is open to anyone.

But understanding the strategy and executing it in a way that actually produces results are two very different things. The fastest path between those two points is learning from someone who has already done it — and done it at a level that removes any doubt about whether the model works.

Michele O'Neil built her Skool community into the number one ranked business community on the platform. She generates eight figures annually using the exact value ladder and community engagement strategies described in this post. She has done it without a massive following, without paid advertising, and without the hustle culture that burns most online entrepreneurs out before they ever reach traction.

Her Community Builders program teaches the entire system — how to launch, how to grow, how to engage, and how to monetize across multiple layers — step by step, with direct access to someone who has walked every stage of the path.

If you are ready to stop trading time for dollars and start building a community that works for you around the clock, this is where to start:

articles.author: Explore Omaha
articles.title: People Do Not Need More Information
articles.body:
What if the thing holding your business back is not your content strategy?

What if it is not your offer, your niche, your posting frequency, or your follower count?

What if the missing piece is something almost no one in the online business space is talking about — and yet it is the exact reason some entrepreneurs quietly build multi-million dollar businesses while others stay stuck producing content that goes nowhere?

According to digital business coach Michele O'Neil, the answer is community. And the distinction she draws between what most business owners are doing and what actually works is worth understanding before you spend another hour creating content no one is engaging with.

---

## People Do Not Need More Information

This is the insight that changes everything once it lands.

Your potential customers are not suffering from a lack of information. They have access to more content, more tutorials, more tips, and more strategies than they could consume in a lifetime. Information is not scarce. Transformation is.

What people actually crave — and what they will pay for, return to, and refer others toward — is connection. They want to feel seen. They want to feel heard. They want to be part of something where their questions get answered, their wins get celebrated, and their struggles get met with real support rather than another video to watch alone.

When a business owner creates that kind of environment, something shifts. The relationship between the business and its customers stops being transactional and starts becoming something people are genuinely loyal to.

That loyalty is where recurring income lives.

---

## What a Community Actually Did for One Business

Drawing from insights shared by Michele O'Neil — a digital business coach whose Skool community became the number one ranked business community on the platform — the impact of building a community space for customers and potential customers was immediate and compounding.

When she launched her business in late 2022, she created a community alongside it — not after success, but from the very beginning. The reason was straightforward: she wanted to understand how her customers were doing and how she could serve them better.

What happened next shaped everything.

Her customers started telling her what they actually wanted. They showed her, through their questions and conversations, what problems they were still facing, what language resonated, and what gaps in the market remained wide open. That feedback loop directly improved her email campaigns, her social content, and her offers.

The result: email open rates above 60 percent across a list of tens of thousands of subscribers. Not because of a clever subject line formula — because she had built genuine trust with people who felt known and valued inside her community.

That is the compounding effect most content strategies can never replicate.

---

## The False Belief Worth Destroying

Here is a limiting belief that keeps most entrepreneurs from even starting a community:

"I need a big audience first."

This is backwards. A community is how you build the audience — not the reward you receive after you already have one.

Michele launched with no following, no reputation in the space, and no roadmap. She built the community first and let the community do what communities naturally do: create connection, generate conversation, and attract more people who resonate with what is being built.

Size is not the goal. Engagement is. A community of five hundred highly engaged people who trust you, learn from you, and buy from you is worth more than fifty thousand passive followers who scroll past your content without a second thought.

The number one ranked community on a major platform is not number one because it is the biggest. It is number one because it is the most engaged. That distinction matters enormously.

---

## Your Community Works When You Do Not

This is the time-freedom piece that most online business builders are chasing without realizing how close they already are to it.

When a community is built and active, it does not stop when you step away. Members answer each other's questions. Conversations continue overnight. New people discover the community through search and referral. Relationships form without your direct involvement in every exchange.

This is fundamentally different from a social media feed, which goes cold the moment you stop posting. A community has its own momentum. It is an asset that appreciates over time rather than a content hamster wheel that demands constant output just to maintain visibility.

For a parent entrepreneur managing a household, a side business, and everything in between, this distinction is not just nice to have. It is the difference between a business that is sustainable and one that burns you out before it ever reaches its potential.

---

## A 30-Minute Daily Outreach Strategy That Adds 40 New Connections Per Day

One of the most practical insights Michele shares with her students is this: she spends just 30 minutes per day on outreach, and that single practice consistently brings an average of 40 new people — customers and interested prospects — into her community daily.

Not 30 minutes of DMs. Not 30 minutes of cold pitching. A targeted, intentional outreach practice built around serving and connecting, not selling.

The principle behind it is simple. When you lead with value, when you are genuinely trying to help people solve a real problem, the conversion happens naturally. You are not chasing people. You are creating a space people want to walk into.

That kind of daily consistency, compounded over months and years, is how a community goes from a handful of members to tens of thousands — and how a business goes from scraping for sales to generating recurring income that does not depend on any single piece of content going viral.

---

## The Bottom Line for Parent Entrepreneurs

If you have been grinding on content — posting every day, tweaking your hooks, chasing the algorithm — and wondering why the results do not match the effort, this framework offers a different answer.

Stop asking how to get more eyeballs on your content. Start asking how to create a space where the people who already found you feel compelled to stay, engage, and bring others in.

Community is not a feature you add to your business after it is already successful. It is the foundation that makes sustained success possible in the first place.

People do not need more content. They need a place to belong.

---

## Take the Next Step

Michele O'Neil's Community Builders program is built specifically to walk entrepreneurs through the process of launching and growing a thriving online community — including the outreach strategy, the engagement systems, and the framework for turning a free community into a recurring income model.

If you are ready to stop producing content into the void and start building something that works for you around the clock, this is the place to start:

[Click here to learn more →](https://www.skool.com/business-and-grant-mentorship-9581/about?ref=b26a59d7ef0b4eca9f8265176094fab3)

---

*You might also like: "You Do Not Need a New Goal — You Need a New Target" — another framework from Michele O'Neil on realigning your business strategy for results.*

---

*Insights like these are part of the ongoing conversation inside Nobility TV Network — a streaming platform built around content that actually matters for families and entrepreneurs. Explore the network: https://nobilitytvnetwork.com*

articles.author: Explore Omaha
articles.title: What a Grant Actually Is
articles.body:
Most people have heard the word "grant" and immediately assumed it was not for them.

Maybe they thought you needed a special background. Maybe they assumed the application process was too complicated. Maybe they simply never had anyone sit down and break down how the system actually works.

Coach K is that person.

She spent over a decade in banking — consumer lending, commercial lending, private lending — watching who got approved for funding and who walked out empty-handed. What she noticed changed the direction of her career: the people who were getting access to capital often did not look like her community. So she made it her mission to change that.

Her own grant journey started with $500. Not glamorous. But it proved something critical — that money was out there, it did not have to be paid back, and consistency was the key to unlocking more of it. Her largest personal grant to date: $146,000, deposited directly into her business bank account.

No credit check. No repayment required.

She shared all of it in a recent episode of the Circle of Greatness podcast with host Nehemiah Davis. Here is what every parent entrepreneur needs to understand.

---

## What a Grant Actually Is

A grant is debt-free funding.

There is no credit score requirement. There is no application fee in most cases. There is no monthly payment waiting for you on the other side. It is money that organizations — corporations, foundations, government agencies, and nonprofits — set aside specifically to invest in businesses and causes they believe in.

Chick-fil-A distributes $5 million in grants annually. They are not looking for perfect credit scores. They are looking for businesses making a measurable difference in their communities. A 10 to 15 minute application telling your story could result in $325,000 walking through your door.

That is not a rumor. That is a program currently open and accepting applicants.

The reason most entrepreneurs miss this is not because they are unqualified. It is because they are not applying.

---

## The Number That Changes Everything

Coach K shared a statistic that reframes the entire conversation around grants.

If the average entrepreneur applies for 21 to 30 grants per month, they have a 99 percent chance of receiving at least one grant.

Read that again.

Twenty to thirty applications per month. That is less than one per day. And the probability of success becomes nearly certain.

She posts five grants every Monday that her community can apply for that week. That alone covers 20 per month. She also recommends a platform called Hello Skip, which allows you to pay a monthly subscription and have it automatically apply for every grant that flows through their system — over one million dollars in grants currently listed on that platform.

The math is simple. If you apply to 240 grants in a year and close just 10 percent of them, that is 24 grants. Close two percent and you still get five. And if one of those five happens to be a $146,000 grant or a $325,000 corporate community investment — the entire year changes.

---

## Getting Your Foundation Right First

Coach K is direct about this: you cannot win money you are not structured to receive.

Before you apply for a single grant, these three things need to be in place.

*Your business plan.* Most entrepreneurs have a vision but never put it on paper. A business plan is not just a document — it is your grant proposal. Without it, you are asking an organization to fund a dream they cannot see. With it, your vision becomes a credible investment.

*Your business address.* This one surprises people. Using your home address on business documents is one of the most common and costly mistakes entrepreneurs make. To a bank or grant organization, a home-based business signals limited overhead needs — which caps the level of funding they consider you eligible for. Coach K's recommendation is to lease space from a friend or colleague who has a commercial building, paying them a small monthly fee to use that address. You get a credible brick-and-mortar presence. They generate a new income stream. Both win.

*Your banking relationship.* Do not walk into a bank asking for $100,000 if you have not even opened a deposit account. Banks use internal point systems — a checking account is a point, a savings account is a point, a credit card is a point. The more products you hold and actively use, the more the bank trusts you. Bring your banker coffee. Introduce yourself. Make yourself a familiar face before you ever need anything. The relationship you build today is what gets someone to fight for your loan approval six months from now.

---

## How to Stand Out When Everyone Is Applying

The question Nehemiah asked that most entrepreneurs are thinking: if hundreds of people are applying for the same grant, how do you separate yourself?

Coach K's answer: the wow factor.

You have roughly ten to fifteen seconds to grab a grant reviewer's attention. In that window, you need to show one thing clearly — your business is making a measurable, real impact on actual people in your community.

Grant organizations are not funding businesses that are looking for a handout. They are investing in businesses that are already solving problems and want help scaling that solution. Your application needs to reflect that. Tell the story of the people your business has helped. Show the change you are creating. Make the reviewer feel the impact before they finish reading the first paragraph.

She also recommends a strategic move that many entrepreneurs overlook: having both a for-profit and a nonprofit entity. Many grants are exclusive to nonprofits. Others are limited to for-profit companies. If you have both, you can apply either way — doubling your eligible opportunities. If starting a nonprofit is not part of your vision, Coach K suggests partnering with an established nonprofit in your space and applying jointly. The connection between your for-profit business and a nonprofit partner is exactly how she structured the $146,000 grant that changed her trajectory.

---

## Grants and Business Credit Together

While grants are the primary focus, Coach K advocates for using both tools strategically.

A line of credit is different from a loan. It sits available as a safety net — you draw from it only when you need it and pay interest only on what you use. It does not sit on your books the way a lump sum loan does. For a growing business, that flexibility matters.

She also recommends credit unions over large national banks for personal service. A smaller institution that actually understands your vision and your community is far more likely to advocate for you during the approval process than a branch where you are an account number among millions.

The goal is not to rely on one tool. It is to build a financial infrastructure — grants for growth, lines of credit for cash flow, banking relationships for long-term leverage — so your business is never fragile.

---

## The Bigger Picture

Coach K's student received $1.7 million in grant funding for a nonprofit that helps single mothers navigate postpartum recovery. That money did not require a perfect credit score. It required a clear mission, a solid structure, and a consistent commitment to applying.

There is money designated right now — by corporations, foundations, and government programs — for businesses doing exactly what you are doing. The only thing standing between you and access to it is whether you are in the game or watching from the sideline.

As Nehemiah put it: being selfish about not going to get that money is not humility. It is leaving resources on the table that could amplify the impact you are already making.

---

## Take the Next Step

Coach K teaches this entire system step by step — from building your foundation to writing your first grant proposal to scaling your applications to 21 to 30 per month.

If you are a business owner who is ready to stop leaving debt-free money on the table, her Business and Grant Mentorship Community is the place to get this done with expert guidance and a community of people who are actively winning grants right now.

Learn more and join here: [Join the Business & Grant Mentorship Community →](https://www.skool.com/business-and-grant-mentorship-9581/about?ref=b26a59d7ef0b4eca9f8265176094fab3)

---

*You might also like: "The Legal Side of Running a Business — What Every Entrepreneur Needs to Have in Place Before Something Goes Wrong" from our Know Your Rights series.*

---

*This conversation was featured on the Circle of Greatness with Nehemiah Davis — one of the shows airing on Nobility TV Network, a streaming platform built around content that moves the needle for families and entrepreneurs. Explore the network: https://nobilitytvnetwork.com*

articles.author: Explore Omaha
articles.title: What Would Happen to Your Family Tonight If You Never Came Home?
articles.excerpt: Not the emotional part. The legal part. If you haven't answered these questions on paper, state law already has the answer — and it doesn't know your family.
articles.body: Not the emotional part. You already know that answer. The legal part. Who gets your accounts? Who decides where your children go? Who makes medical decisions if you are alive but cannot speak? Who controls your business? Who pays your bills while everything gets sorted out? Most people have never answered those questions on paper. And the terrifying truth is — if you have not — someone else already has the answer waiting. It is called state law. And it does not know you, it does not know your family, and it does not care what you would have wanted. --- *Unpreparedness is not a personal failure. But it is a personal choice.* --- ## FALSE BELIEF #1: "I don't have enough assets to need a will." This is one of the most common reasons people put off estate planning. And it makes complete sense on the surface. A will feels like something for wealthy people — people with estates, investment portfolios, real estate holdings. If you are living paycheck to paycheck or just getting started, a will can feel like overkill. Here is the truth. A will is not primarily about money. It is about your children. If you have minor children and you die without a will, a court — not you, not your family, not your closest friend — decides who raises them. That court follows a legal process. It considers many factors. It does not consider the conversation you had with your sister five years ago about how you would want her to step in. It does not consider that your brother-in-law is not the right fit. It does not know your values, your faith, your parenting philosophy, or your wishes. You could have $0 in your bank account and still desperately need a will if you have a child. *A will is not a wealth document. It is a love document.* --- ## FALSE BELIEF #2: "My spouse will automatically handle everything." It feels logical. You are married. Of course your spouse gets everything and handles everything if something happens to you. That is just how it works. Except in many states, that is not actually how the law works. In states that follow intestate succession laws — which is every state when there is no will — assets are often divided between a surviving spouse and children according to a legal formula. Depending on the state and the situation, a surviving spouse may not receive full control of the estate. Assets held only in your name may require probate. Bank accounts, retirement accounts, and life insurance policies with no named beneficiary can be tied up in legal proceedings for months. And here is the part nobody talks about: even if your spouse does receive everything legally, without a healthcare power of attorney in place, they may face legal barriers to making medical decisions on your behalf if you are incapacitated but not deceased. Hospitals follow legal protocols. Good intentions do not override legal documentation. *What feels automatic is often anything but.* --- ## FALSE BELIEF #3: "Getting legal documents done is expensive and complicated." This one keeps more families unprotected than any other false belief. And it used to be true. Traditionally, a complete estate plan — will, living will, healthcare power of attorney, financial power of attorney — required multiple appointments with an estate attorney, document preparation fees, and a bill that could run $1,000 to $3,000 or more depending on the complexity and the market. For a family already stretched thin, that cost is a real barrier. So it goes on the list. The someday list. The when-we-have-more-money list. Here is what changed. Legal protection plans now exist that include full estate planning document preparation as part of a monthly membership — a membership that costs a fraction of a single traditional attorney visit. The documents are drafted by real attorneys. They are legally valid. And they can be accessed through a mobile app or desktop, on your schedule. The barrier that kept millions of families unprotected has been removed. *Preparation is no longer a privilege. It is a choice.* --- Here is the soundbite worth remembering today: *Delayed protection is a decision too.* Every day that passes without these documents in place is not a neutral day. It is a day your family carries risk you could have removed. The decision to wait is still a decision. --- This post is not here to sell you anything. It is here to ask you one question that most people spend their whole lives avoiding: If tonight was the last night — is your family protected? If the answer made you uncomfortable, keep reading. The next post in this series breaks down exactly what four documents every family needs, how to get them done without paying traditional attorney rates, and what each one actually protects. *Because the families who prepare are not the ones who fear the worst. They are the ones who love their people enough to plan for it.* --- ## Ready to Protect Your Family? LegalShield gives families access to real attorneys for a low monthly rate — including full estate planning document preparation. Will, living will, healthcare power of attorney, financial power of attorney. Done right. Done affordably. [👉 Get Protected with LegalShield →](https://allanpettit.legalshieldassociate.com/). To learn more: click HERE.

events.title: Gretna Days

events.description: The 89th Annual Sarpy County Fair runs July 29 – August 2, 2026 in Springfield, NE. Five days of 4-H/FFA shows and exhibits, a carnival with unlimited-ride wristbands, truck & tractor pulls, rodeo, live music, and a Sunday demolition derby. Ages: all ages. Faith-based: no. Cost: grounds admission free; carnival wristbands ~$30; individual grandstand events $10–$25. Full run: July 29 – August 2, 2026.
events.title: Sarpy County Fair & Rodeo
events.price: Free admission; carnival & grandstand events ticketed
events.time: Wed–Sun; carnival opens midday/evening (see daily schedule)

events.description: The Washington County Fair runs July 31 – August 5, 2026 in Arlington, NE (about 35 minutes north of Omaha). A family-friendly county fair with 4-H/FFA exhibits and livestock shows, a carnival, live entertainment, and grandstand events. Ages: all ages. Faith-based: no. Cost: most activities free; some grandstand events ticketed. Full run: July 31 – August 5, 2026.
events.title: Washington County Fair

events.description: Bellevue's premier community celebration, presented by the Bellevue-Offutt Kiwanis Club, August 13–15, 2026. Includes a library presentation (Aug 13), a free community meal (Aug 14), and a Saturday parade plus Washington Park festivities with a pancake feed, foam party, bounce houses, music, and vendors (Aug 15). Ages: all ages. Faith-based: no. Cost: free; some food events ticketed. Full run: August 13–15, 2026.
events.title: Arrows to Aerospace Celebration
events.time: Aug 13 (library presentation), Aug 14 (community meal), Aug 15 (parade & park festivities)

events.description: New for 2026, the City of Papillion's Mayor's Concert Series at SumTur Amphitheater features The Jimmy Weber Band (country) on Friday, July 3 at 7:30 PM and Mojo & the Bayou Gypsies (zydeco/Cajun) on Saturday, July 25 at 7:30 PM. Family-friendly live music at the metro's premier outdoor venue. This listing's date reflects the final concert (July 25) so it stays visible. Ages: all ages. Faith-based: no. Cost: ~$10 in person / $12 online per show.
events.title: Mayor's Summer Concert Series at SumTur Amphitheater

events.description: A guided evening Full Moon Hike at Fontenelle Forest in Bellevue on Friday, July 31, 2026 (the next full-moon hike follows on Aug 28). Explore the forest trails by moonlight with a naturalist. Good for families and all ages comfortable with an evening walk. Faith-based: no. Cost: admission/registration applies — see official site. Single evening event.
events.title: Full Moon Hike at Fontenelle Forest

events.description: Every Tuesday from June through August 2026, The Durham Museum stays open late (5–8 PM) with $5 admission for all ages (members may bring a guest for $5). Explore the historic Union Station and current exhibits in the evening. Ongoing weekly program; this listing's date reflects the final Tuesday of the run (Aug 25) so it stays visible. Ages: all ages. Faith-based: no. Cost: $5 admission after 5 PM on Tuesdays.
events.title: Happily Ever After Hours: $5 After 5 at The Durham Museum

events.address: Maplewood Lanes, West Lanes, Spare Time & Papio Bowl (Papillion) — see site
events.description: Free summer bowling for kids 15 and under — two free games per day at participating Omaha-area centers, all summer long (May 1 – September 30, 2026). Register each child online; an optional paid adult/family pass covers grown-ups. A great rainy-day family activity. Ages 15 & under. Faith-based: no.
events.title: Kids Bowl Free — Summer 2026
events.time: Runs May 1 – September 30, 2026 · 2 free games per day

events.venue: Downtown Ralston & Liberty First Credit Union Arena
events.description: Ralston's 66th annual Independence Day Celebration (July 3-4, 2026) — one of the metro's largest. Family highlights: a children's parade, the big community parade, a downtown street dance with face painters and a strolling magician, rock climbing, a fireworks show, and an after-parade foam party with bouncy slides. Free, all ages. Faith-based: no.
events.title: Ralston Independence Day Celebration
events.time: July 3: kids activities, picnic & fireworks at 10 PM · July 4: children's parade 10 AM, community parade 1 PM, foam party 3:30 PM

events.description: Free outdoor storytime for young children at rotating Omaha parks on select summer Fridays (around 10 AM) — stories, songs, movement, and early-literacy fun led by Omaha Public Library staff. Best for toddlers through early elementary. Check the OPL events calendar for the current week's park location. Faith-based: no.
events.title: Out & About Storytime with Omaha Public Library

events.description: A free, faith-based community celebration for the whole family featuring live music, games, local food trucks, and a fireworks show. Free admission, all ages. Faith-based: yes.
events.title: Christ Community Church Summer Celebration
events.time: Evening — live music, games, food trucks & fireworks

events.description: Website maintenance
events.title: Maintenance Test Post

events.description: Watch jaw-dropping aerial performances from military jets and civilian stunt pilots set against the stunning backdrop of Standing Bear Lake. Families can enjoy food vendors, military displays, and interactive exhibits on the ground while thrilling aerobatics light up the skies above west Omaha. Free to attend.
events.title: Omaha Air Show at Standing Bear Lake

events.description: The beloved Nelson Produce Farm in Valley transitions from its Sunflower Festival into full Fall Festival mode. Expect u-pick pumpkins, hayrack rides, a giant hay pyramid, over 50 pumpkin varieties, an apple cider slushie bar, caramel apples, donuts, and homemade syrups from the Coffee Barn. A perfect family farm day just 20 minutes from Omaha.
events.title: Nelson Produce Farm — Fall Festival Opens

events.description: Omaha's beloved swing dance festival returns to South 24th Street for a full weekend of live big band music, swing dancing workshops, and community fun. Whether you're a seasoned dancer or a curious beginner, the Cowtown Jamborama is a one-of-a-kind Omaha experience the whole family will enjoy watching.
events.title: Cowtown Jamborama — Swing Dance Festival

events.description: Voted Nebraska's Best Pumpkin Patch, Skinny Bones opens for its 18th season in Blair, just 30 minutes from Omaha. Attractions include a massive corn maze, hayrides, pumpkin picking, gem mining, a bounce pillow, kettle corn, caramel apples, and dozens of photo-worthy spots. Great for kids of all ages — runs through November 1.
events.title: Skinny Bones Pumpkin Patch — Season Opens

events.description: Celebrate the apple harvest at one of Nebraska's most beloved fall traditions at Arbor Day Farm in Nebraska City, just 45 minutes from Omaha. Sample dozens of apple varieties, fresh-pressed cider, apple cider donuts, and explore the stunning grounds of Arbor Day Farm. Live music, kids activities, and fall shopping make this a perfect family day trip.
events.title: AppleJack Festival — Arbor Day Farm

events.description: One of the largest fall festivals in the country kicks off its 2026 season! Vala's in Gretna features hundreds of pumpkin varieties, an apple orchard, cider making, corn mazes, hayrides, live entertainment, and dozens of family activities spread across 160 acres. A must-do Omaha fall tradition for the whole family.
events.title: Vala's Pumpkin Patch — Opening Weekend

events.description:
Kids build a take-home wooden project, get a free apron, patch, and goggles — all at no cost. Registration is required and spots fill fast. Find participating Omaha-area locations:

• Omaha (Central): 7677 Dodge St
• Omaha (Northwest): 1010 N 102nd St
• Omaha (Southwest): 17005 Wright Plaza
• Council Bluffs: 3200 Manawa Centre Dr

Use the Lowe's Workshop Finder to lock in your spot.
events.title: First Saturday Free Kids Workshop — Lowe's

events.description: The final summer edition of Dundee's monthly neighborhood street music series. Local musicians perform in the heart of Dundee on the third Saturday evening. A wonderful send-off to summer in one of Omaha's most charming neighborhoods.
events.title: Dundee Street Music — September Edition

events.description: Tunes on Town Square is Ralston's free community concert series, held the third Thursday of each month from May through September, 6 to 8 PM, at the new gazebo in downtown Ralston Town Square. Bring a chair and some snacks and enjoy a relaxed evening of live local music with friends and neighbors. Free admission, all ages welcome. Please note: per city ordinance, no alcoholic beverages may be consumed in public. July's featured musical guest is The Day Traders. 2026 lineup: May 21 – High Heel, June 18 – Back Alley, July 16 – The Day Traders, August 20 – One 2 Go, September 17 – Taxi Driver.
events.title: Tunes on Town Square — The Day Traders

events.description: Enjoy an evening soundtrack at the market by some of the region's most talented local musicians. The Beats & Bites Stage features a rotating lineup of solo acts and crowd favorites bringing acoustic sets, soulful originals, and covers. Bring a blanket, grab something delicious, and settle in for a relaxed evening under the sky.
events.title: Beats & Bites at the Farmers Market

events.description: An immersive walking murder mystery experience produced by Omaha Performing Arts. Follow the clues through the Holland Campus as you try to solve the mystery. Tickets required through o-pa.org. Running June 2–21, 2026.
events.title: Clue: A Walking Mystery — Omaha Performing Arts

events.description: Tunes on Town Square is Ralston's free community concert series, held the third Thursday of each month from May through September, 6 to 8 PM, at the new gazebo in downtown Ralston Town Square. Bring a chair and some snacks and enjoy a relaxed evening of live local music with friends and neighbors. Free admission, all ages welcome. Please note: per city ordinance, no alcoholic beverages may be consumed in public. September's featured musical guest is Taxi Driver. This final concert of the season is also part of the Heartland Plein Air Festival's Paint Around Ralston Night — come watch professional artists at work while you enjoy the music. 2026 lineup: May 21 – High Heel, June 18 – Back Alley, July 16 – The Day Traders, August 20 – One 2 Go, September 17 – Taxi Driver.
events.title: Tunes on Town Square — Taxi Driver (Paint Around Ralston Night)

events.description: The legendary New Orleans Hot 8 Brass Band comes to Omaha's Miller Park for a high-energy free outdoor show. Presented in partnership with Nelson Mandela Elementary School and the Minne Lusa and Miller Park Neighborhood Associations. Lawn opens at 5 PM, pre-show at 6:30 PM, show at 7:30 PM.
events.title: Hot 8 Brass Band at Miller Park

events.description: Kids and teens who made their Summer Reading Program goals can redeem their Storm Chaser voucher for a free berm seat at an Omaha Storm Chasers Triple-A baseball game! A great family outing at Werner Park to celebrate young readers.
events.title: Omaha Storm Chasers — Summer Reading Program Reward Game

events.description: Third Saturday live music in the heart of Dundee, 6–8 PM. A different local Omaha-area musician every month, performing right in the neighborhood streets. Stroll, shop, grab dinner, and enjoy the community music happening right outside.
events.title: Dundee Street Music — August Edition

events.description: Join the City of Gretna at Gretna Crossing Park Amphitheater for a FREE community concert with incredible local talent. Gates open at 6 PM. Music from 7 PM–10 PM. Bring the whole family for a night of live music just southwest of Omaha.
events.title: Gretna Crossing Park — Music at the Crossing

events.description: Tunes on Town Square is Ralston's free community concert series, held the third Thursday of each month from May through September, 6 to 8 PM, at the new gazebo in downtown Ralston Town Square. Bring a chair and some snacks and enjoy a relaxed evening of live local music with friends and neighbors. Free admission, all ages welcome. Please note: per city ordinance, no alcoholic beverages may be consumed in public. August's featured musical guest is One 2 Go. 2026 lineup: May 21 – High Heel, June 18 – Back Alley, July 16 – The Day Traders, August 20 – One 2 Go, September 17 – Taxi Driver.
events.title: Tunes on Town Square — One 2 Go

events.description: Join the City of Fremont at John C. Fremont City Park for an outdoor concert series. Kids' activities begin at 6:30 PM, followed by live music from 7 to 9 PM. A family-friendly evening in a beautiful park setting, just 35 miles from Omaha.
events.title: Concert in the Park — Fremont City Park

events.description: Free In-Store Kids Workshops at Lowe's every first Saturday of the month. Join at 9 AM while supplies last. Kids build take-home projects with real tools and materials. A fun, hands-on STEM activity for children and families.
events.title: First Saturday Free Kids Workshop — Lowe's

events.description: Benson Boone brings his 'Wanted Man Tour' to Omaha. The breakout artist is one of the most exciting live performers of the moment. Don't miss this night at CHI Health Center.
events.title: Benson Boone: Wanted Man Tour

events.description: Head over to the Performance Pavilion in Gene Leahy Mall for a powerful free performance by the U.S. Navy Band and Omaha Symphonic Winds. Patriotic, inspiring, and completely free — a perfect evening for the whole family at the heart of Omaha's revitalized riverfront park.
events.title: Navy Band & Omaha Symphonic Winds — Gene Leahy Mall

events.description: Rotating live music at the Omaha Farmers Market. Local and regional musicians perform acoustic sets, folk, soul, and originals while you browse the market's local produce, food trucks, and vendors. Bring a blanket for a perfect Friday evening.
events.title: Beats & Bites at the Farmers Market

events.description: Nancy Gillis from Nebraska Humanities speaks on the origins, history, and current state of the five tribes of Nebraska: Omaha, Ponca, Pawnee, Santee, and Winnebago. Of Cherokee/Choctaw descent, Gillis brings a lifetime of expertise to this important cultural conversation. Free, registration recommended.
events.title: Nebraska Tribes Lecture — Nebraska Humanities

events.description: Last chance to see the incredible Dinosaurs Around the World: Passport to Pangea exhibition at Lauritzen Gardens before it closes September 7. Thirteen life-sized animatronic dinosaurs roar through the garden's 100 acres. Tickets required.
events.title: Dinosaurs Around the World — Final Weeks at Lauritzen

events.description: Free monthly hands-on kids workshop at Lowe's. Every first Saturday at 9 AM. Children build take-home projects with real tools. While supplies last. Great early-morning family activity.
events.title: First Saturday Free Kids Workshop — Lowe's

events.address: S 192nd St & W Dodge Rd, Omaha, NE 68022
events.description: Saturday mornings belong to the kids! Heartwood Kids Club is a free weekly program filled with hands-on fun designed especially for children ages 4–8. Activities include crafts, demonstrations, creative projects, and interactive experiences — each week brings a new surprise. Kids must be accompanied by an adult. Running every Saturday June 13 – September.
events.title: Heartwood Kids Club — Free Saturday Morning Program

events.description: Third week of the fan-favorite Jazz on the Green outdoor series at Midtown Crossing, presented by Omaha Performing Arts. All ages welcome. Bring lawn chairs and enjoy dinner from nearby restaurants.
events.title: Jazz on the Green — Week 3

events.description: Artists Oscar Rios Pohirieth and Amado Espinoza perform music from the Andean mountain region of South America, with a special program inspired by animals. Part of Art-Tastic Saturdays at The Joslyn. Performance 12–1 PM; explore all activities 11 AM–2 PM.
events.title: Nebraska Crossroads Music Festival at The Joslyn

events.description: The grand finale of this summer's Jazz on the Green series at Midtown Crossing, presented by Omaha Performing Arts. End the summer the right way — live music, warm evenings, and great community.
events.title: Jazz on the Green — Final Week

events.description: Enjoy FREE popular family movies under the stars at SumTur Amphitheater. Gates and concessions open at 7:30 PM. This season, bands from the Omaha Blues Society Blues Education Program provide pre-show music. Movies and dates subject to change. Bring chairs, blankets, and the whole family.
events.title: SumTur Outdoor Theatre — Free Movie Nights

events.description: On Monday nights from May 4 through September 14, guests and their canine companions are invited to explore Lauritzen Gardens' 100 acres together from 5 to 8 PM. A beloved Omaha tradition for families and pet owners alike.
events.title: Leashes at Lauritzen — Monday Evening Dog Walks

events.description: The record-breaking musical phenomenon returns to Omaha. Wicked tells the incredible untold story of the witches of Oz. Don't miss this limited engagement at the historic Orpheum Theater.
events.title: Wicked — Broadway at the Orpheum

events.description: The second Thursday of Omaha Performing Arts' beloved outdoor Jazz on the Green series at Midtown Crossing. Bring a blanket, enjoy the lawn, and let the music carry the evening. Park opens at 5 PM.
events.title: Jazz on the Green — Week 2

events.description: Step into a prehistoric adventure at Lauritzen Gardens with Dinosaurs Around the World: Passport to Pangea. This immersive traveling exhibition features 13 life-sized animatronic dinosaurs that roar and move through the garden's 100 stunning acres. Running May 30 – September 7, 2026. A must-do family experience this summer.
events.title: Dinosaurs Around the World at Lauritzen Gardens

events.description: Omaha Conservatory of Music guests bring the magic of music alive with a free musical storytime! Includes a special musical story, songs with movement, and an instrument petting zoo. Perfect for young children and music-curious families.
events.title: Omaha Conservatory Musical Storytime

events.description: The City of Omaha Parks and Recreation Department presents Live on the Lawn, a FREE family-friendly summer concert series at parks across the metro. Bring your chairs, blankets, and enjoy live local music in your neighborhood park.
events.title: Live on the Lawn — City of Omaha Free Concert Series

events.description: Enjoli & Timeless bring smooth vocals, big grooves, and undeniable stage presence to the Music at Miller Park summer concert series. Classic soul and modern R&B flair, heartfelt slow jams, and dance-ready hits for the whole family. Free and open to the community.
events.title: Music at Miller Park — Summer Concert

events.address: S 192nd St & W Dodge Rd, Omaha, NE 68022
events.description: Heartwood Kids Club weekly hands-on program for children ages 4–8. Crafts, demonstrations, creative projects, and interactive experiences every Saturday morning through September. Kids must be accompanied by an adult. Free and open to the public.
events.title: Heartwood Kids Club — Free Saturday Morning Program

events.description: Another great Thursday evening of live music at Village Pointe Amphitheater. The Vibes Summer Concert Series features local and regional artists performing rock, pop, funk, and soul. Bring lawn chairs, grab dinner from nearby restaurants, and enjoy a perfect summer night.
events.title: Vibes Summer Concert Series at Village Pointe

events.description: Get ready to soak up the sun and feel the rhythm at the Summer Vibes Concert Series at Village Pointe Amphitheater. Select Thursday evenings from 6:30–8:30 PM featuring local and regional talent — rock, pop, funk, and soul. Seating area opens at 4 PM. All ages welcome.
events.title: Vibes Summer Concert Series at Village Pointe

events.description: Step into the magic of Disney and discover iconic looks from beloved Disney characters in this enchanting showcase of nearly 70 mesmerizing ensembles spanning five decades of Disney film and television. An up-close experience for all ages — running through late August.
events.title: Heroes & Villains: The Art of the Disney Costume

events.description: Playing with Fire, Inc. presents another summer of free live music concerts in Omaha. Guaranteed admission tickets available online. Subscribe for lineup updates and enjoy a full evening of music in a community setting. All ages welcome.
events.title: Playing with Fire Free Summer Concert Series

events.description: Grab your friends and family for a fun-filled evening at Stinson Park in Aksarben Village. Pack your chairs, blankets, and coolers and settle in for a great night of live music. Come hungry, come ready to dance, and most importantly — come have fun!
events.title: Stinson Park Concert — Aksarben Village

events.description: Join Nebraska Wildlife Rehab at the Omaha Public Library for an interactive look into the world of wildlife rehabilitation. Learn what the NWR team does, what it takes to become a wildlife rehabilitator, and how animals end up needing care. Explore each step of the rehabilitation journey. For patrons ages 6+. Registration required.
events.title: Wildlife Rehabilitation Program — Nebraska Wildlife Rehab

events.description: Grab your family and friends and join at Zorinsky Lake for an awesome show with The Personics! Bring your lawn chairs, blankets, coolers, and dancing shoes for this free outdoor evening concert at one of Omaha's most beautiful parks.
events.title: Zorinsky Lake Park Free Concert — The Personics

events.description: Experience live music like never before at Lauritzen Gardens' beautiful new Oasis and Amphitheater. Surrounded by natural beauty, enjoy a summer of unforgettable performances from tribute bands to crowd favorites. Tickets required. Check lauritzengardens.org for the full lineup.
events.title: Outdoor Concerts at the Oasis — Lauritzen Gardens Amphitheater

events.description: Another spectacular evening of live music at Lauritzen Gardens' stunning Oasis and Amphitheater. Surrounded by beautiful gardens, enjoy unforgettable summer performances from tribute bands and crowd-favorite artists. A uniquely Omaha evening out.
events.title: Outdoor Concerts at the Oasis — Lauritzen Gardens Amphitheater

events.description: Omaha's most beloved free outdoor concert series returns. Presented by Omaha Performing Arts, Jazz on the Green fills Midtown Crossing with world-class live jazz every Thursday evening. Bring a blanket and enjoy the warm Omaha night. Park opens for seating at 5 PM, music starts at 7:30 PM.
events.title: Jazz on the Green — Midtown Crossing

events.description: Join the Omaha Symphony with Legends of Country at the Performance Pavilion in Gene Leahy Mall for a spectacular free outdoor concert. Blankets and folding chairs can be placed starting at 3:00 PM. Concert begins at 8:30 PM. One of the summer's premier free events.
events.title: RiverFront Community Concert — Omaha Symphony

events.description: The Vibes Summer Concert Series continues at Village Pointe Amphitheater. Free live music every Thursday evening featuring talented local and regional performers. Seating opens at 4 PM, music from 6:30–8:30 PM. Family and pet friendly.
events.title: Vibes Summer Concert Series at Village Pointe

events.description: Make summer movie season magical! Head to Marcus Theaters between June 7 and August 13 for a series of family favorites — a different movie each week for only $3 (+tax). Grab the family for an affordable summer outing at the movies.
events.title: Marcus Theaters Summer $3 Family Film Series

events.address: S 192nd St & W Dodge Rd, Omaha, NE 68022
events.description: Celebrate America's 250th Independence Day at Heartwood Beach Club! The park transforms into a beach-themed playground with giant sand sculptures, sand sculpting lessons, giant water slides, games, festive food, a rocking concert at sunset, and one of the best fireworks viewing spots in the metro. Wear your red, white, and blue!
events.title: Fourth of July at Heartwood — Beach Party & Fireworks

events.description: Free live music on the patio every Wednesday at Telegraph Distilling. Ice-cold drinks and tasty eats available from The Mill. Bring the family, bring the pets, bring a chair! Lineup includes Church of Blues (July 1), Radiostar (July 8), Raw Nerve (July 15), Jarana (July 22), Downtown Collective (July 29).
events.title: Live at Telegraph — Free Patio Music Series

events.address: 8116 Park View Blvd, La Vista, NE 68128
events.description: Gather in Central Park to celebrate La Vista's community and its first responders. This safety-focused event features touch-a-truck with first responder vehicles, a greeting from the Mayor, face painters, activities, free treats, safety information, and demonstrations. Great for little ones!
events.title: La Vista Community Safety Fair & Touch-a-Truck

events.description: Fourth week of the beloved Jazz on the Green outdoor series at Midtown Crossing. One of the best free summer nights in Omaha. Bring the whole family and enjoy live jazz under the stars.
events.title: Jazz on the Green — Week 4

events.description: Dundee is bringing the neighborhood to life with monthly live music in the streets. On the third Saturday evening of each month, 6–8 PM, enjoy local talent performing right in the heart of the Dundee neighborhood. Whether you're out for a stroll or heading to dinner, pause and soak in the community energy that makes Dundee special.
events.title: Dundee Street Music — Monthly Neighborhood Concert

events.description: Join Omaha Public Library at The Joslyn Art Museum for storytime featuring stories, creative movement, music, and exploration of art. Storytime is geared toward preschoolers but all ages are welcome. Part of the Joslyn's monthly Art-Tastic Saturdays series.
events.title: Art-Tastic Saturdays at The Joslyn

events.address: 11001 N John J Pershing Dr, Omaha, NE 68112
events.description: Paddle along the Missouri River as the sun sets over Omaha. Guided tour suitable for beginners and families with kids 8+.
events.title: Sunset Kayaking on the Missouri

events.venue: Gene Leahy Mall at The RiverFront
events.description: A celebration of the longest day of the year with live performances, art installations, local food, and family activities throughout Gene Leahy Mall.
events.title: Summer Solstice Festival

events.description: Explore the gardens with the whole family. Enjoy nature walks, scavenger hunts, and interactive botanical activities for kids of all ages.
events.title: Family Fun Day at Lauritzen Gardens

events.description: Fresh produce, artisan goods, live music, and food trucks. A Saturday morning tradition in the Old Market district.
events.title: Omaha Farmer's Market

events.description: Bring a blanket and enjoy world-class jazz under the stars at Midtown Crossing. Family-friendly atmosphere with food vendors nearby.
events.title: Jazz on the Green

events.description: Classic independent cinema paired with dinner at a nearby Dundee restaurant. Special prix fixe menu available for the evening.
events.title: Date Night: Dinner & Film at Dundee Theater
